_source_note: Reconstructed from public reporting (Congressional Research Service, Kiel Institute, LNRG Technology, Cashu Research, discoveryalert.com.au) for offline backtesting. Severities/confidences below are this project's own estimates based on described event magnitude, not scores taken from those sources.

events.summary: Renewed US sanctions pressure on Iranian oil exports escalates diplomatic tension in the Gulf.
Reports of naval posturing near the Strait of Hormuz as US-Iran tension intensifies.
Escalation to direct military engagement; tanker loadings begin slowing sharply.
Tanker transit through the Strait falls to a trickle; Gulf producers begin curtailing output.
Near-total disruption to Hormuz transit; IEA characterizes it as the largest supply disruption in the history of the world oil market.
Early signs of de-escalation talks, though transit remains severely restricted.
Partial reopening reported; physical market tightness expected to persist for months.
